Back to 1956-01 (844 obs verified live 2026-06-10 — 2026-04 = 1.1%). Replaces frozen FRED CPGRLE01JPM659N / OECD MEI mirror (stale at 2021-06). Wired §3.1.3 follow-up: blended into JP_INFL1 alongside JPN_CPI_YOY headline.

Indicator: Japan CPI (All Items, Index) — Statistics Bureau 2020-base
Notes: §2.A A13 tier-0 upgrade (2026-07-09). Statistics Bureau of Japan (MIC) 2020-base Consumer Price Index — 消費者物価指数（2020年基準）, e-Stat toukei 00200573, statsDataId 0003427113. The true PRIMARY for the Japan CPI index level; supersedes the OECD COICOP2018 index mirror (now T1, base 2015=100) in macro_library_dbnomics.csv via the tier merge. Slice pinned via getMetaInfo 2026-07-09: cdTab=1 (表章項目 指数 = index measure; the other measures are 2=前月比・前年比 and 3=前年同月比 YoY), cdCat01=0001 (2020年基準品目 総合 = all-items headline; item dimension has 790 classifications), cdArea=00000 (地域 全国 = nationwide). lang=J required (table absent from the English DB; lang=J in the query string overrides fetch_series' default lang=E). Verified live via adapter 2026-07-09: 733 obs 1970-01 -> 2026-05 = 113.5 (base 2020=100 confirmed; 2020 avg ≈ 100). Correctly ends 2026-05 for the national all-items series — the June 2026 figure visible in the getMetaInfo time dimension is the Tokyo-ward lead cut (cdArea=13A01, published ~1 month ahead); national June releases ~2026-07-19. freshness_override_days=90: national CPI publishes ~3 weeks after month-end, so the latest obs runs ~50-80d old between refreshes; 90 keeps it in a healthy band rather than tripping EXPIRED.

DB.nomics NBS/M_A0D01/A0D0102 — National Bureau of Statistics of China: Money & Quasi-Money (M2) Supply, Growth Rate. Despite the NBS column title 'same period last year=100', values are served as YoY percent (e.g. 9.0 @ 2026-02, 8.5 @ 2025-12). Replaces frozen FRED OECD-MEI mirror MYAGM2CNM189N (a stock LEVEL mislabelled "Percent Change YoY", froze 2019-08) and resolves the long-standing China M2 accepted-gap — see forward_plan §2.B / chronology (PR #221). NBS mirror lags ~4 months (override 120). Verified live 2026-06-17 (9.0% @ 2026-02).

Indicator: France MFI rate — new loans to households for house purchase
Notes: §2.A A6 resolved 2026-07-09 (credentialed BDF_API_KEY probe). Shiller's headline CAPE (P/E10) ratio computed from S&P 500 real price ÷ 10-year average real earnings. Sourced from the Yale ie_data.xls workbook (http://www.econ.yale.edu/~shiller/data/ie_data.xls). Long-history monthly back to 1881. Wired §3.13 2026-06-10 — parser verified offline against the documented sheet layout; live cross-validation pending the first credentialed CI run (sandbox blocks Yale + the shillerdata.com / datahub.io / posix4e mirrors).

Monthly S&P Composite price level from the Yale ie_data.xls workbook. Long-history common-frame counterpart to daily SPX tickers; back to Jan 1871. Wired §3.13 2026-06-10 — cross-validation pending live CI run.
Monthly S&P Composite trailing dividend from the Yale ie_data.xls workbook. Back to Jan 1871. Wired §3.13 2026-06-10 — cross-validation pending live CI run.

Monthly S&P Composite trailing earnings from the Yale ie_data.xls workbook. Back to Jan 1871. Used as the denominator anchor for the CAPE definition. Wired §3.13 2026-06-10 — cross-validation pending live CI run.

Long-run US CPI from the Yale ie_data.xls workbook (BLS CPI-U linked to Warren-Pearson back to 1871). Pre-1913 portion is not BLS-native but Shiller's standard splice; intended as a pre-WW2 cross-validation anchor for regime-AA Phase 0c, NOT a replacement for the modern BLS CUSR0000SA0 column (which remains the canonical USA_CPI_INDEX). Wired §3.13 2026-06-10.

Long-run US 10-year Treasury yield from the Yale ie_data.xls workbook (FRED GS10 spliced to NBER long-rate series pre-1953). Pre-1953 portion is not FRED-native; intended as a pre-WW2 cross-validation anchor for regime-AA Phase 0c. Wired §3.13 2026-06-10.

Kenneth French data library — US market excess return back to 1926-07 from the F-F 3-Factor monthly file. ZIP: https://mba.tuck.dartmouth.edu/pages/faculty/ken.french/ftp/F-F_Research_Data_Factors_CSV.zip. Wired §3.11 2026-06-10 — driven by regime-AA Phase 0 long-run factor requirement. Switched 2026-06-11 from the 5-Factors_2x3 file (which only starts 1963-07) to the 3-Factor file to honour the 1926-07 history brief. RMW/CMA stay on the 5-factor ZIP (they only exist from 1963-07).

Small-minus-Big size factor from the F-F 3-Factor monthly file (ZIP F-F_Research_Data_Factors_CSV.zip). History from 1926-07. Note: the 3-Factor SMB is computed on the 2x3 size/book-to-market sort and differs slightly from the 5-Factor SMB which averages three 2x3 sorts (size/BM + size/OP + size/Inv) — we use the 3-Factor version to preserve the 1926-07 history.
High-minus-Low book/market value factor from the F-F 3-Factor monthly file (ZIP F-F_Research_Data_Factors_CSV.zip). History from 1926-07.

Robust-minus-Weak operating-profitability factor from the F-F 5-Factor 2x3 monthly file (ZIP F-F_Research_Data_5_Factors_2x3_CSV.zip). History from 1963-07 (RMW post-dates Mkt/SMB/HML — operating-profitability data unavailable earlier).
Conservative-minus-Aggressive investment factor from the F-F 5-Factor 2x3 monthly file (ZIP F-F_Research_Data_5_Factors_2x3_CSV.zip). History from 1963-07.
One-month Treasury-bill return packaged with the F-F 3-Factor monthly file (ZIP F-F_Research_Data_Factors_CSV.zip). History from 1926-07.

Indicator: Atlanta Fed GDPNow — US Real GDP Growth (Q/Q SAAR)
Notes: Atlanta Fed GDPNow real-time nowcast of US Q/Q annualised real GDP growth. Source: GDPTrackingModelDataAndForecasts.xlsx on the GDPNow homepage (https://www.atlantafed.org/cqer/research/gdpnow — canonical xlsx path verified 2026-06-11 by scraping the page's Downloads section). Updated 5-10 times per quarter on weekdays following key data releases (ISM, FT900, Retail Trade, Wholesale Trade, Construction Spending, Housing Starts, Durable Goods, Personal Income & Outlays, IP, Existing-Home Sales). The raw cadence is irregular within-quarter and silent between quarters; we declare frequency=Weekly because the compute_macro_market calculator resamples to weekly Friday (matching the rest of the macro_market spine) and forward-fills between updates. freshness_override_days=14 because the model is silent between the final-nowcast-of-Q and initial-nowcast-of-Q+1 days (typically a ~3-5 business-day window around BEA's advance estimate); 14 days catches a stale endpoint without false-positives during the normal quiet windows. Wired §3.1.4 2026-06-11 — first §3.1.4 nowcast via a NEW primary-source fetcher.

Indicator: New York Fed Staff Nowcast — US Real GDP Growth (Q/Q SAAR)
Notes: New York Fed Staff Nowcast real-time nowcast of US Q/Q annualised real GDP growth. Source: New-York-Fed-Staff-Nowcast_download_data.xlsx on the Staff Nowcast homepage (https://www.newyorkfed.org/research/policy/nowcast — canonical xlsx path verified 2026-06-11 by SERP-resolving the page's Downloads section). Published weekly on Fridays during tracking quarters; silent for ~3-5 business days around BEA's advance estimate between the final-nowcast-of-Q and initial-nowcast-of-Q+1 windows. The raw cadence is weekly with quiet windows; we declare frequency=Weekly because the compute_macro_market calculator resamples to weekly Friday (matching the rest of the macro_market spine) and forward-fills between updates. freshness_override_days=14 mirrors US_GDPNOW — same nowcast quiet-window pattern. Second §3.1.4 nowcast fetcher; provides the US_NOWCAST1 second-opinion read on US growth alongside US_GDPNOW1 (Atlanta Fed GDPNow). Wired §3.1.4 2026-06-11.
